skill.name: create-unit-test
skill.description: Create and run unit tests following the project's architecture and guidelines (Robolectric, naming, location).
skill.summary: Create Kotlin unit tests that follow project naming, location, Robolectric, and Gradle test conventions.
skill.author: AnySoftKeyboard

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged markdown backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but most are false positives from prose, placeholders, and file paths. The only confirmed risk is that the skill instructs agents to run Gradle test commands, which can execute repository build logic. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Gradle Test Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Gradle test commands. This is expected for unit test creation, but Gradle can execute repository build scripts and project code, so users should review the repository before running tests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and are intended to be executed. The context is legitimate testing workflow, with no evidence of malicious arguments, networking, or secret access.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at naming, path, and guideline examples. These are markdown inline code spans, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain placeholders, paths, and source set names inside markdown. They do not contain a Ruby program or any shell backtick execution context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms at descriptive text lines. No cryptographic function, hashing algorithm, encryption API, or security-sensitive code appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe unit testing guidance only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported reconnaissance at Robolectric and deterministic testing guidance. The text does not collect host data, enumerate the system, or inspect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain testing framework and reliability advice. No commands or instructions gather host information or user data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository Build Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Running Gradle tests may execute build scripts controlled by the repository. This pattern is common for development workflows but should be treated carefully for untrusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Gradle commands are explicit and executable. The risk depends on the trust level of the target repository, not on malicious content in the skill.

content.value_statement: Android projects need tests that match local architecture, naming, and runner rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through focused Kotlin unit test creation and execution.
actual_capabilities: Plans happy path, error case, and edge case coverage before implementation.
Places Kotlin test files in the correct module test source set.
Applies Robolectric guidance when testing Android components.
Encourages fakes before mocks for maintainable test doubles.
Runs module or project unit tests with Gradle commands.
Uses test result XML paths to diagnose failures.
limitations: Focused on unit tests, not broad instrumentation test workflows.
Assumes the repository uses Gradle and Android test conventions.
Requires project context to select the correct module and package path.
Running tests can execute build scripts from the target repository.

use_cases.title: Add coverage for a Kotlin class
use_cases.description: Create focused unit tests for a class while matching the repository naming and package layout.

use_cases.title: Test Android component behavior
use_cases.description: Use Robolectric guidance when a unit test needs Android framework resources or lifecycle behavior.

use_cases.title: Stabilize failing unit tests
use_cases.description: Inspect test results, identify failure causes, and adjust tests toward deterministic behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic unit test
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a unit test for this Kotlin class. Follow the project test location, naming, and Arrange-Act-Assert style.

prompt_templates.title: Plan test coverage first
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this class and propose happy path, error case, and edge case tests before writing the unit test file.

prompt_templates.title: Use Robolectric where needed
prompt_templates.prompt: Create unit tests for this Android component. Use Robolectric if framework resources or lifecycle behavior are required.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose and repair failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the relevant Gradle unit test task, inspect the failure report, and update the test to be deterministic.

output_examples.input: Create tests for a Kotlin preferences helper.
output_examples.output: A new test file is placed in the module test source set with clear cases for default values, saved values, and invalid input.

output_examples.input: Add coverage for an Android activity helper.
output_examples.output: The result uses Robolectric guidance, provides required resources, and explains which behavior each test verifies.

output_examples.input: A new unit test fails in CI.
output_examples.output: The response points to the test result report, summarizes the failure, and suggests a deterministic fix.

best_practices: Review build scripts before running Gradle commands in an unfamiliar repository.
Ask for behavior expectations before adding implementation-specific assertions.
Prefer fakes when they keep tests readable and stable.
anti_patterns: Do not place ordinary unit tests in the instrumentation test source set.
Do not mock simple collaborators when a small fake is clearer.
Do not add flaky assertions that depend on timing, order, or external state.

faq.question: What type of tests does this skill create?
faq.answer: It focuses on local unit tests for Android and Kotlin projects.

faq.question: When should Robolectric be used?
faq.answer: Use Robolectric when a unit test needs Android framework behavior, resources, or lifecycle support.

faq.question: Does the skill run tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Gradle unit test commands for modules and full project checks.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the suggested commands?
faq.answer: Use normal repository trust checks first. Gradle commands can execute project build logic.

faq.question: Does it prefer mocks or fakes?
faq.answer: It prefers fakes and uses mocks only when fakes would be too complex.

faq.question: Can it fix failing tests?
faq.answer: It can inspect test result reports and guide deterministic fixes for failures.
